title: capelio
intro: Capelio, commonly known as Capillipedium parviflorum, is a type of grass-like plant that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to reduce stress and improve sleep quality. The therapeutic actions of Capelio include its ability to calm the nervous system, reduce anxiety, and promote relaxation, making it a popular natural remedy for stress relief. The bioactive constituents of Capelio include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties and make it a valuable plant in traditional medicine.
side_effects: Capelio can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and digestive issues. Ingesting the plant's toxic compounds can lead to symptoms like nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can cause skin burns, blisters, and even permanent scarring. Improper handling of the capelio plant can also trigger respiratory problems, including shortness of breath and asthma attacks.
